@@ -0,0 +1,206 @@
+/**
+ * Email notification adapter — delivers notifications via email.
+ *
+ * This is a fully implemented email adapter with:
+ * - Template rendering: Generates HTML and plain-text email bodies
+ *   based on notification type (order confirmation, welcome, etc.)
+ * - Recipient validation: Validates email address format before sending.
+ * - Configurable delivery: Stub mode for development/testing, production
+ *   mode for real SMTP/API integration.
+ *
+ * Implements the NotificationAdapter interface. Registered with the
+ * NotificationService to handle all notifications on the 'email' channel.
+ *
+ * Configuration is injected via constructor to support different
+ * environments (dev, staging, production).
+ */
+import type { NotificationAdapter } from './notification.adapter.js';
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationType } from '../models/notification.js';
+/**
+ * A rendered email template containing both HTML and plain-text versions.
+ */
+export interface RenderedEmail {
+    /** HTML version of the email body. */
+    html: string;
+    /** Plain-text version of the email body. */
+    text: string;
+    /** The subject line (may be enhanced by the template). */
+    subject: string;
+}
+/**
+ * A complete email message ready for delivery.
+ */
+export interface EmailMessage {
+    /** Sender email address. */
+    from: string;
+    /** Recipient email address. */
+    to: string;
+    /** Email subject line. */
+    subject: string;
+    /** HTML body content. */
+    html: string;
+    /** Plain-text body content. */
+    text: string;
+    /** When the email was composed. */
+    composedAt: Date;
+    /** The notification type that generated this email. */
+    notificationType: NotificationType;
+    /** Additional headers or metadata. */
+    metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+/**
+ * Configuration for the email adapter.
+ */
+export interface EmailAdapterConfig {
+    /** SMTP host or email API endpoint. */
+    host: string;
+    /** SMTP port (e.g., 587 for TLS). */
+    port: number;
+    /** Sender email address for the "From" field. */
+    fromAddress: string;
+    /** Whether this is a stub (logs instead of sending). */
+    stubMode: boolean;
+    /** Company name used in email templates. */
+    companyName: string;
+    /** Maximum retries for failed delivery attempts. */
+    maxRetries: number;
+}
+/**
+ * Default configuration for development/testing.
+ */
+export declare const defaultEmailConfig: EmailAdapterConfig;
+/**
+ * Validates an email address format.
+ *
+ * Uses a reasonable regex that covers the vast majority of valid email
+ * addresses without being overly permissive. Checks:
+ * - Has exactly one @ symbol
+ * - Local part is non-empty and uses allowed characters
+ * - Domain has at least one dot and valid characters
+ * - Domain TLD is at least 2 characters
+ *
+ * @param email - The email address to validate.
+ * @returns true if the email format is valid.
+ */
+export declare function isValidEmail(email: string): boolean;
+/**
+ * Resolves a recipient identifier to a normalised string.
+ *
+ * Trims whitespace and normalises to lowercase. If the recipient looks
+ * like a valid email address, it is returned as-is. If it doesn't look
+ * like an email (e.g., a user ID like "user-1"), it is still returned
+ * but marked as unvalidated — the caller can decide how to handle it.
+ *
+ * Returns null only if the input is empty or not a string.
+ *
+ * @param recipient - The recipient string from the notification payload.
+ * @returns An object with the normalised recipient and whether it's a valid email, or null if empty.
+ */
+export declare function resolveRecipient(recipient: string): {
+    address: string;
+    isValidEmail: boolean;
+} | null;
+/**
+ * Renders an email template for the given notification payload.
+ *
+ * Each notification type has a dedicated template that generates both
+ * HTML and plain-text versions of the email. The templates include
+ * proper formatting, branding, and contextual content based on the
+ * notification type and metadata.
+ *
+ * @param payload - The notification payload to render.
+ * @param companyName - The company name for branding.
+ * @returns A rendered email with HTML, text, and subject.
+ */
+export declare function renderEmailTemplate(payload: NotificationPayload, companyName: string): RenderedEmail;
+/**
+ * Email notification adapter.
+ *
+ * Sends notifications via email with full template rendering and
+ * recipient validation. In stub mode (default), logs the email details
+ * and records them in memory instead of actually delivering.
+ *
+ * Pipeline:
+ * 1. Validate recipient email address format.
+ * 2. Render notification into HTML + plain-text email templates.
+ * 3. Compose complete EmailMessage (from, to, subject, body).
+ * 4. Deliver via stub (log + record) or production (SMTP/API).
+ * 5. Return success/failure result (never throws).
+ */
+export declare class EmailAdapter implements NotificationAdapter {
+    readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+    private logger;
+    private config;
+    private sentEmails;
+    constructor(config?: Partial<EmailAdapterConfig>);
+    /**
+     * Send a notification via email.
+     *
+     * Full pipeline:
+     * 1. Validates the recipient email address.
+     * 2. Renders notification type-specific HTML/text templates.
+     * 3. Composes the complete email message.
+     * 4. Delivers via stub (records in memory) or production mode.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload to deliver via email.
+     * @returns Result indicating success or failure. Never throws.
+     */
+    send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+    /**
+     * Get all emails sent in stub mode. Useful for testing.
+     *
+     * Returns defensive copies of the recorded email messages,
+     * including the rendered HTML and plain-text content.
+     */
+    getSentEmails(): EmailMessage[];
+    /**
+     * Clear sent email records. Useful for testing.
+     */
+    clearSentEmails(): void;
+    /**
+     * Get the current adapter configuration.
+     */
+    getConfig(): Readonly<EmailAdapterConfig>;
+    /**
+     * Render a template for a given payload without sending.
+     * Useful for previewing emails or testing templates in isolation.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload to render.
+     * @returns The rendered email content (HTML, text, subject).
+     */
+    renderTemplate(payload: NotificationPayload): RenderedEmail;
+    /**
+     * Validate a recipient email address without sending.
+     *
+     * @param recipient - The email address to validate.
+     * @returns true if the email address is valid.
+     */
+    validateRecipient(recipient: string): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Attempt email delivery with retry logic.
+     *
+     * Retries up to `config.maxRetries` times on transient failures.
+     * Throws on permanent failure after all retries are exhausted.
+     *
+     * @param email - The complete email message to deliver.
+     * @throws Error if delivery fails after all retries.
+     */
+    private deliverWithRetry;
+    /**
+     * Real email delivery — override in production or replace with actual SMTP/API.
+     *
+     * In a real deployment, this method would:
+     * - Open an SMTP connection to `config.host:config.port`
+     * - Authenticate with credentials
+     * - Send the email message
+     * - Close the connection
+     *
+     * @param _email - The email message to deliver.
+     * @throws Error if delivery fails.
+     */
+    private deliverEmail;
+    /**
+     * Simple delay utility for retry backoff.
+     */
+    private delay;
+}

+/**
+ * Base interface for all notification adapters.
+ *
+ * CONSTRAINT: Every notification delivery channel (email, SMS, webhook, in-app)
+ * MUST implement this interface. The NotificationService dispatches payloads
+ * to adapters based on the `channel` property.
+ *
+ * Adapter contract:
+ * 1. `channel` must match exactly one NotificationChannel value.
+ * 2. `send()` must be async and return a NotificationResult.
+ * 3. `send()` must NOT throw — return { success: false, failureReason } instead.
+ * 4. Adapters are stateless; they do not store notifications.
+ * 5. Adapters receive their configuration via constructor injection.
+ *
+ * Example implementation:
+ * ```typescript
+ * class EmailAdapter implements NotificationAdapter {
+ *   readonly channel: NotificationChannel = 'email';
+ *   async send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult> {
+ *     // ... send email ...
+ *     return { success: true, notificationId: payload.subject, channel: 'email' };
+ *   }
+ * }
+ * ```
+ */
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult } from '../models/notification.js';
+/**
+ * Interface that all notification delivery adapters must implement.
+ */
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+    /** The delivery channel this adapter handles. Must be unique per adapter. */
+    readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+    /**
+     * Attempt to deliver a notification.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload to deliver.
+     * @returns A result indicating success or failure. Must not throw.
+     */
+    send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

+/**
+ * Event bus for decoupled inter-module communication.
+ *
+ * Architecture decision: This application uses an event-driven pattern
+ * for communication between services. Instead of direct function calls
+ * between modules, services emit events that other services can listen to.
+ *
+ * This promotes loose coupling and makes it easy to add new behaviors
+ * (e.g., new notification channels) without modifying existing services.
+ */
+import type { AppEvent } from './event-types.js';
+export type EventHandler<T extends AppEvent = AppEvent> = (event: T) => void | Promise<void>;
+export declare class EventBus {
+    private handlers;
+    private eventLog;
+    /**
+     * Subscribe to events of a given type.
+     */
+    on<T extends AppEvent>(eventType: T['type'], handler: EventHandler<T>): () => void;
+    /**
+     * Emit an event to all registered handlers.
+     */
+    emit<T extends AppEvent>(event: T): Promise<void>;
+    /**
+     * Get all emitted events. Useful for testing and debugging.
+     */
+    getEventLog(): AppEvent[];
+    /**
+     * Clear all handlers and event log.
+     */
+    clear(): void;
+}

+ * - user:*   — User lifecycle events (emitted by UserService)

+ *
+ * ALL cross-service communication MUST go through the EventBus.

+ */

+ * Architecture: All notifications flow through the EventBus. When a domain

+ * Type hierarchy:
+ * - NotificationType: What happened (e.g., order_confirmation)
+ * - NotificationChannel: How to deliver (e.g., email, sms)
+ * - NotificationPayload: What to send (constructed by NotificationService)
+ * - NotificationRecord: What's stored (includes delivery status)
+ * - NotificationResult: What adapters return (success/failure)

+/**
+ * User notification preferences model.
+ *
+ * Controls how and when a user receives notifications. Currently focused on
+ * email settings since email is the only notification channel. Designed to
+ * be extensible when additional channels (SMS, webhook, in_app) are added.
+ *
+ * Preference hierarchy:
+ * - UserNotificationPreferences: Top-level container keyed by user ID.
+ * - EmailPreferences: Email-specific settings (enabled, frequency, categories).
+ * - NotificationCategoryPreference: Per-category opt-in/out for each notification type.
+ *
+ * Design notes:
+ * - Users who have no stored preferences receive all notifications (opt-out model).
+ * - The `enabled` flag on EmailPreferences is a master switch — if false, no
+ *   email notifications are sent regardless of category settings.
+ * - Frequency controls how often digest-style emails are batched. 'immediate'
+ *   means every notification is sent as it occurs.
+ */
+import type { NotificationType } from './notification.js';
+/**
+ * Email delivery frequency.
+ * - 'immediate': Send each notification as it occurs.
+ * - 'daily': Batch notifications into a daily digest.
+ * - 'weekly': Batch notifications into a weekly digest.
+ */
+export type EmailFrequency = 'immediate' | 'daily' | 'weekly';
+/**
+ * Per-category notification preference.
+ * Maps a notification type to whether the user wants to receive it.
+ */
+export interface NotificationCategoryPreference {
+    /** The notification type this preference applies to. */
+    category: NotificationType;
+    /** Whether the user wants to receive this category of notification. */
+    enabled: boolean;
+}
+/**
+ * Email-specific notification preferences.
+ */
+export interface EmailPreferences {
+    /** Master switch: if false, no email notifications are sent. */
+    enabled: boolean;
+    /** How frequently email notifications are delivered. */
+    frequency: EmailFrequency;
+    /** Per-category preferences. Categories not listed default to enabled. */
+    categoryPreferences: NotificationCategoryPreference[];
+}
+/**
+ * Complete user notification preferences.
+ */
+export interface UserNotificationPreferences {
+    /** Unique preferences ID (e.g., pref-0001). */
+    id: string;
+    /** The user ID these preferences belong to. */
+    userId: string;
+    /** Email notification settings. */
+    email: EmailPreferences;
+    /** When the preferences were created. */
+    createdAt: Date;
+    /** When the preferences were last updated. */
+    updatedAt: Date;
+}
+/**
+ * Input for creating user notification preferences.
+ * All fields are optional — defaults are applied by the service.
+ */
+export interface CreatePreferencesInput {
+    userId: string;
+    email?: Partial<EmailPreferences>;
+}
+/**
+ * Input for updating existing user notification preferences.
+ * Only the fields provided will be changed.
+ */
+export interface UpdatePreferencesInput {
+    email?: Partial<EmailPreferences>;
+}
+/**
+ * Default email preferences for new users.
+ * Opt-out model: all notifications enabled by default.
+ */
+export declare function getDefaultEmailPreferences(): EmailPreferences;

+/**
+ * User notification preferences model.
+ *
+ * Controls how and when a user receives notifications. Currently focused on
+ * email settings since email is the only notification channel. Designed to
+ * be extensible when additional channels (SMS, webhook, in_app) are added.
+ *
+ * Preference hierarchy:
+ * - UserNotificationPreferences: Top-level container keyed by user ID.
+ * - EmailPreferences: Email-specific settings (enabled, frequency, categories).
+ * - NotificationCategoryPreference: Per-category opt-in/out for each notification type.
+ *
+ * Design notes:
+ * - Users who have no stored preferences receive all notifications (opt-out model).
+ * - The `enabled` flag on EmailPreferences is a master switch — if false, no
+ *   email notifications are sent regardless of category settings.
+ * - Frequency controls how often digest-style emails are batched. 'immediate'
+ *   means every notification is sent as it occurs.
+ */
+/**
+ * Default email preferences for new users.
+ * Opt-out model: all notifications enabled by default.
+ */
+export function getDefaultEmailPreferences() {
+    return {
+        enabled: true,
+        frequency: 'immediate',
+        categoryPreferences: [
+            { category: 'order_confirmation', enabled: true },
+            { category: 'order_status_update', enabled: true },
+            { category: 'welcome', enabled: true },
+            { category: 'account_updated', enabled: true },
+        ],
+    };
+}

+/**
+ * Notification service — orchestrates notification delivery in response to domain events.
+ *
+ * ARCHITECTURE DECISION: Event-Driven with Adapter Dispatch
+ * =========================================================
+ * This service uses the EventBus exclusively for receiving domain events.
+ * ALL cross-service communication flows through EventBus — no direct callbacks,
+ * no shared mutable state, no CallbackRegistry.
+ *
+ * Flow:
+ * 1. A domain service (OrderService, UserService) emits an event via EventBus.
+ * 2. NotificationService listens for relevant events.
+ * 3. Event handlers map domain events → NotificationPayload(s).
+ * 4. Payloads are dispatched to the appropriate NotificationAdapter by channel.
+ * 5. Delivery results are recorded as NotificationRecords.
+ * 6. notification:sent / notification:failed events are emitted for observability.
+ *
+ * CONSTRAINTS:
+ * - All adapters MUST implement the NotificationAdapter interface.
+ * - All domain events MUST flow through EventBus.emit().
+ * - Adapters MUST NOT throw from send() — return { success: false } instead.
+ * - This service owns the notification lifecycle; adapters own delivery mechanics.
+ *
+ * Depends on:
+ * - EventBus (event system) for receiving ALL domain events
+ * - NotificationAdapter implementations for delivery
+ * - PreferencesService (optional) for checking user notification preferences
+ */
+import type { EventBus } from '../events/event-bus.js';
+import type { NotificationAdapter } from '../adapters/notification.adapter.js';
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationRecord, CreateNotificationInput } from '../models/notification.js';
+import type { PreferencesService } from './preferences.service.js';
+/** Reset the ID counter. Used in tests. */
+export declare function resetNotificationIdCounter(): void;
+export declare class NotificationService {
+    private eventBus;
+    private logger;
+    private notifications;
+    private adapters;
+    private preferencesService;
+    constructor(eventBus: EventBus);
+    /**
+     * Set the preferences service for checking user notification preferences.
+     *
+     * This is optional — if not set, all notifications are sent (backwards
+     * compatible with existing behaviour).
+     *
+     * @param preferencesService - The preferences service instance.
+     */
+    setPreferencesService(preferencesService: PreferencesService): void;
+    /**
+     * Register a notification adapter for a delivery channel.
+     *
+     * Only one adapter per channel is allowed. Registering a second adapter
+     * for the same channel replaces the previous one.
+     *
+     * @param adapter - The adapter to register. Must implement NotificationAdapter.
+     */
+    registerAdapter(adapter: NotificationAdapter): void;
+    /**
+     * Unregister the adapter for a delivery channel.
+     *
+     * @param channel - The channel whose adapter to remove.
+     * @returns true if an adapter was removed, false if none was registered.
+     */
+    unregisterAdapter(channel: NotificationChannel): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Get all registered adapter channels.
+     */
+    getRegisteredChannels(): NotificationChannel[];
+    /**
+     * Send a notification through the appropriate adapter.
+     *
+     * This is the core dispatch method. It:
+     * 1. Builds a NotificationRecord from the input.
+     * 2. Looks up the adapter for the specified channel.
+     * 3. Calls adapter.send() and records the result.
+     * 4. Emits notification:sent or notification:failed events.
+     *
+     * @param input - Notification details. Channel defaults to 'email'.
+     * @returns The created NotificationRecord with delivery status.
+     */
+    send(input: CreateNotificationInput): Promise<NotificationRecord>;
+    /**
+     * Register event handlers for all notification-worthy domain events.
+     *
+     * IMPORTANT: ALL event subscriptions go through EventBus.on().
+     * Do NOT use CallbackRegistry or direct callbacks.
+     */
+    private registerEventHandlers;
+    /**
+     * Handle order created — send confirmation email to the ordering user.
+     */
+    private handleOrderCreated;
+    /**
+     * Handle order status change — notify user of the update.
+     */
+    private handleOrderStatusChanged;
+    /**
+     * Handle user created — send welcome notification.
+     */
+    private handleUserCreated;
+    /**
+     * Handle user updated — notify user of account changes.
+     */
+    private handleUserUpdated;
+    /**
+     * Get all notification records.
+     */
+    getNotifications(): NotificationRecord[];
+    /**
+     * Get notifications for a specific recipient.
+     */
+    getNotificationsForRecipient(recipient: string): NotificationRecord[];
+    /**
+     * Get notifications filtered by status.
+     */
+    getNotificationsByStatus(status: NotificationRecord['status']): NotificationRecord[];
+    /**
+     * Get notifications filtered by type.
+     */
+    getNotificationsByType(type: NotificationRecord['type']): NotificationRecord[];
+    /**
+     * Get the total count of notification records.
+     */
+    getNotificationCount(): number;
+    /**
+     * Clear all notification records. Useful for testing.
+     */
+    clearNotifications(): void;
+}

index 0000000..44ed9b4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/dist/services/preferences.service.d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,101 @@
+/**
+ * Preferences service — business logic for user notification preferences.
+ *
+ * Manages CRUD operations on notification preferences and provides a
+ * query interface for the NotificationService to check whether a given
+ * notification should be delivered to a user.
+ *
+ * Design decisions:
+ * - Opt-out model: users without stored preferences receive all notifications.
+ * - The `email.enabled` flag is a master switch; if false, all email
+ *   notifications are suppressed regardless of category settings.
+ * - Categories not explicitly listed in `categoryPreferences` default to enabled.
+ * - This service does NOT emit events — it is a passive data service queried
+ *   by the NotificationService during dispatch.
+ *
+ * Depends on:
+ * - PreferencesRepository (data layer) for persistence
+ */
+import type { PreferencesRepository } from '../repositories/preferences.repository.js';
+import type { UserNotificationPreferences, CreatePreferencesInput, UpdatePreferencesInput } from '../models/user-preferences.js';
+import type { NotificationType, NotificationChannel } from '../models/notification.js';
+/** Reset the ID counter. Used in tests. */
+export declare function resetPreferencesIdCounter(): void;
+export declare class PreferencesService {
+    private preferencesRepository;
+    private logger;
+    constructor(preferencesRepository: PreferencesRepository);
+    /**
+     * Create notification preferences for a user.
+     *
+     * If the user already has preferences, throws an error. Use
+     * `updatePreferences` to modify existing preferences.
+     *
+     * @param input - The preferences to create. Defaults are applied for omitted fields.
+     * @returns The created preferences.
+     */
+    createPreferences(input: CreatePreferencesInput): UserNotificationPreferences;
+    /**
+     * Get notification preferences for a user.
+     *
+     * @param userId - The user ID to look up.
+     * @returns The user's preferences, or undefined if none are stored.
+     */
+    getPreferences(userId: string): UserNotificationPreferences | undefined;
+    /**
+     * Update notification preferences for a user.
+     *
+     * Only the provided fields are changed; all others remain as-is.
+     *
+     * @param userId - The user whose preferences to update.
+     * @param input - The fields to update.
+     * @returns The updated preferences.
+     * @throws Error if no preferences exist for the user.
+     */
+    updatePreferences(userId: string, input: UpdatePreferencesInput): UserNotificationPreferences;
+    /**
+     * Delete notification preferences for a user.
+     *
+     * After deletion, the user will receive all notifications (default behaviour).
+     *
+     * @param userId - The user whose preferences to delete.
+     * @returns true if preferences were deleted, false if none existed.
+     */
+    deletePreferences(userId: string): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Get or create preferences for a user.
+     *
+     * If the user has no stored preferences, creates default preferences.
+     * This is a convenience method for the notification check flow.
+     *
+     * @param userId - The user ID.
+     * @returns The user's preferences (existing or newly created defaults).
+     */
+    getOrCreatePreferences(userId: string): UserNotificationPreferences;
+    /**
+     * Check whether a notification should be sent to a user on a given channel.
+     *
+     * This is the primary query used by the NotificationService before dispatching.
+     * It checks:
+     * 1. Whether the channel is enabled (master switch).
+     * 2. Whether the specific notification category is enabled.
+     *
+     * Users without stored preferences are assumed to want all notifications
+     * (opt-out model).
+     *
+     * @param userId - The user to check.
+     * @param channel - The notification channel (currently only 'email').
+     * @param notificationType - The type of notification being sent.
+     * @returns true if the notification should be sent, false if suppressed.
+     */
+    shouldSendNotification(userId: string, channel: NotificationChannel, notificationType: NotificationType): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Check whether a specific email notification should be sent based on
+     * user preferences.
+     *
+     * @param preferences - The user's stored preferences.
+     * @param notificationType - The notification type to check.
+     * @returns true if the email should be sent.
+     */
+    private shouldSendEmail;
+}

+/**
+ * Callback registry for direct service-to-service notification.
+ *
+ * Alternative approach to EventBus: register callbacks directly on services
+ * for lightweight, synchronous notification without an intermediary.
+ *
+ * Trade-offs vs EventBus:
+ * - Simpler (no event types, no serialization)
+ * - Synchronous by default (predictable ordering)
+ * - Tighter coupling (caller knows about callbacks)
+ * - No event log / replay capability
+ */
+export declare class CallbackRegistry<T = unknown> {
+    private callbacks;
+    /**
+     * Register a callback to be notified when events occur.
+     */
+    register(cb: (data: T) => void): () => void;
+    /**
+     * Notify all registered callbacks with the given data.
+     */
+    notify(data: T): void;
+    /**
+     * Get the number of registered callbacks.
+     */
+    get size(): number;
+    /**
+     * Clear all registered callbacks.
+     */
+    clear(): void;
+}
+import type { OrderStatusChangedEvent } from '../events/event-types.js';
+/**
+ * Registry for order status change callbacks.
+ * Services register here to be notified of status changes
+ * via the direct callback pattern (as opposed to EventBus).
+ */
+export declare const statusChangeCallbacks: CallbackRegistry<OrderStatusChangedEvent>;

+/**
+ * Simple in-memory database for the application.
+ *
+ * Provides basic CRUD operations with collection-based storage.
+ * This is the utility layer — the deepest dependency in the architecture.
+ *
+ * Architecture note: All data access goes through Repository classes,
+ * which depend on this Database class. Direct database access from
+ * services is an anti-pattern in this codebase.
+ */
+export interface DatabaseRecord {
+    id: string;
+    [key: string]: unknown;
+}
+export declare class Database {
+    private collections;
+    /**
+     * Get or create a collection.
+     */
+    private getCollection;
+    /**
+     * Insert a record into a collection.
+     */
+    insert(collection: string, record: DatabaseRecord): DatabaseRecord;
+    /**
+     * Find a record by ID.
+     */
+    findById(collection: string, id: string): DatabaseRecord | undefined;
+    /**
+     * Find all records in a collection, optionally filtered by a predicate.
+     */
+    findAll(collection: string, predicate?: (record: DatabaseRecord) => boolean): DatabaseRecord[];
+    /**
+     * Update a record by ID. Merges the update into the existing record.
+     */
+    update(collection: string, id: string, update: Partial<DatabaseRecord>): DatabaseRecord;
+    /**
+     * Delete a record by ID.
+     */
+    delete(collection: string, id: string): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Count records in a collection.
+     */
+    count(collection: string): number;
+    /**
+     * Clear all data from all collections.
+     */
+    clear(): void;
+}
+export declare function getDatabase(): Database;
+export declare function resetDatabase(): void;

+/**
+ * Pagination utilities for list endpoints.
+ */
+export interface PaginationResult<T> {
+    /** Items on the current page */
+    items: T[];
+    /** Current page number (1-indexed) */
+    page: number;
+    /** Number of items per page */
+    pageSize: number;
+    /** Total number of items across all pages */
+    totalItems: number;
+    /** Total number of pages */
+    totalPages: number;
+    /** Whether there is a next page */
+    hasNext: boolean;
+    /** Whether there is a previous page */
+    hasPrevious: boolean;
+}
+/**
+ * Paginate an array of items.
+ *
+ * @param items - The full array of items to paginate
+ * @param page - The page number to retrieve (1-indexed)
+ * @param pageSize - Number of items per page
+ * @returns Paginated result with metadata
+ */
+export declare function paginate<T>(items: T[], page: number, pageSize: number): PaginationResult<T>;

+/**
+ * User notification preferences model.
+ *
+ * Controls how and when a user receives notifications. Currently focused on
+ * email settings since email is the only notification channel. Designed to
+ * be extensible when additional channels (SMS, webhook, in_app) are added.
+ *
+ * Preference hierarchy:
+ * - UserNotificationPreferences: Top-level container keyed by user ID.
+ * - EmailPreferences: Email-specific settings (enabled, frequency, categories).
+ * - NotificationCategoryPreference: Per-category opt-in/out for each notification type.
+ *
+ * Design notes:
+ * - Users who have no stored preferences receive all notifications (opt-out model).
+ * - The `enabled` flag on EmailPreferences is a master switch — if false, no
+ *   email notifications are sent regardless of category settings.
+ * - Frequency controls how often digest-style emails are batched. 'immediate'
+ *   means every notification is sent as it occurs.
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationType } from './notification.js';
+
+/**
+ * Email delivery frequency.
+ * - 'immediate': Send each notification as it occurs.
+ * - 'daily': Batch notifications into a daily digest.
+ * - 'weekly': Batch notifications into a weekly digest.
+ */
+export type EmailFrequency = 'immediate' | 'daily' | 'weekly';
+
+/**
+ * Per-category notification preference.
+ * Maps a notification type to whether the user wants to receive it.
+ */
+export interface NotificationCategoryPreference {
+  /** The notification type this preference applies to. */
+  category: NotificationType;
+  /** Whether the user wants to receive this category of notification. */
+  enabled: boolean;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Email-specific notification preferences.
+ */
+export interface EmailPreferences {
+  /** Master switch: if false, no email notifications are sent. */
+  enabled: boolean;
+  /** How frequently email notifications are delivered. */
+  frequency: EmailFrequency;
+  /** Per-category preferences. Categories not listed default to enabled. */
+  categoryPreferences: NotificationCategoryPreference[];
+}
+
+/**
+ * Complete user notification preferences.
+ */
+export interface UserNotificationPreferences {
+  /** Unique preferences ID (e.g., pref-0001). */
+  id: string;
+  /** The user ID these preferences belong to. */
+  userId: string;
+  /** Email notification settings. */
+  email: EmailPreferences;
+  /** When the preferences were created. */
+  createdAt: Date;
+  /** When the preferences were last updated. */
+  updatedAt: Date;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Input for creating user notification preferences.
+ * All fields are optional — defaults are applied by the service.
+ */
+export interface CreatePreferencesInput {
+  userId: string;
+  email?: Partial<EmailPreferences>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Input for updating existing user notification preferences.
+ * Only the fields provided will be changed.
+ */
+export interface UpdatePreferencesInput {
+  email?: Partial<EmailPreferences>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Default email preferences for new users.
+ * Opt-out model: all notifications enabled by default.
+ */
+export function getDefaultEmailPreferences(): EmailPreferences {
+  return {
+    enabled: true,
+    frequency: 'immediate',
+    categoryPreferences: [
+      { category: 'order_confirmation', enabled: true },
+      { category: 'order_status_update', enabled: true },
+      { category: 'welcome', enabled: true },
+      { category: 'account_updated', enabled: true },
+    ],
+  };
+}
